commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The PR needs contributor runtime proof and a maintainer choice on whether Worktrunk is mandatory or a compatibility fallback is required; this should stay human-gated rather than queued for automated repair.; Needs attention: The patch introduces an unpinned external lifecycle executable for local dev startup.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve a fallback when Worktrunk is unavailable — package.json:38 (sha=044c9b8a1d42ce5b8d19251938fb4f4ca010ee71)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Worktrunk config and `.worktreeinclude`, routes `dev:worktree` through `wt --yes dev`, adds an env-only setup mode, and terminates managed dev children by process group.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main blocker from source inspection: `package.json` hard-calls `wt --yes dev`, while the PR head package and lockfile do not provide that executable. I did not run the startup command because it would spawn local services.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor runtime proof and a maintainer choice on whether Worktrunk is mandatory or a compatibility fallback is required; this should stay human-gated rather than queued for automated repair.

target.title: chore: route worktree startup through Worktrunk
target.body:
## Summary

- add Worktrunk project config for ClawHub linked-worktree setup/start/stop lifecycle
- use `wt step copy-ignored` with `.worktreeinclude` to copy `node_modules` into new worktrees, then fall back to `bun install`
- keep `seed:dev` as a normal app command while routing `dev:worktree` through Worktrunk
- make managed local dev child processes killable as a process group so Convex does not linger after seed/dev cleanup

## Validation

- `wt --yes setup`
- `wt --yes dev`
- `wt --yes stop`
- `bun run seed:dev`
- `wt switch --create pe/wt-smoke-... --base=@ --no-cd --format json`
- `wt remove -f -D --foreground pe/wt-smoke-...`
- `VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bunx vitest run scripts/setup-worktree.test.ts scripts/dev-worktree.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run ci:static`

## Comparison

Compared with the handrolled preflight branch, this net change is `7 files changed, 48 insertions, 352 deletions`: Worktrunk owns lifecycle hooks, branch-hashed ports, dependency copying, and cleanup; ClawHub keeps only app-specific env/Convex validation and startup behavior.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
